Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1 follows the required sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since approved cases must finalize documentation before close case and case 1 does so case 1 does not violate normative rules

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2 shows Risk Assessment before Credit Check and later Loan Decision Rejected followed by Finalize Documentation and then Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Because credit check must occur before risk assessment case 2 violates the ordering rule and because rejected cases must not finalize documentation case 2 also violates the rejection logic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 3 follows the strict control flow Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Although the timing is delayed the mandatory order is preserved so case 3 does not violate normative rules

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The desiderata state that credit check should ideally occur within 24 hours inform applicant should ideally occur within 48 hours of loan decision and a follow up call is encouraged before closing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 fails the desiderata because the credit check occurs more than 24 hours after application the applicant is informed later than the recommended 48 hours and there is no follow up call

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 1 appears to satisfy the recommended timing expectations and there is no evidence of suboptimal behavior in the log

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: A possible interpretation for case 3 is operational delay backlog or difficulty reaching the applicant which would explain the desiderata deviations without implying a hard rule violation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: A possible interpretation for case 2 is process error training issue or incorrect event recording but under the given log it remains a normative violation
